content_of_post_to_comment:
Hi everyone,

I'm a 25-year-old guy from Tamil Nadu, India. I'm trying to become a much better version of myself, and I'd really appreciate advice from people who have gone through a similar journey.

To be honest, I've been a lazy person for a long time. I struggle with focus, discipline, consistency, and procrastination. When I work, my mind gets distracted easily, and I often feel satisfied once the main part of a task is done instead of finishing everything to a high standard.

I'm planning to follow this routine for the next 90 days:

Wake up early every day.

Do yoga or go to the gym.

Focus on work for 5–8 hours using deep work sessions with short breaks.

Avoid doomscrolling on social media.

Take cold showers.

Read books every day.

Quit smoking.

Stay away from porn.

My career

I worked as a .NET and Angular developer for about 5 years. Three months ago, I switched to the ML/AI field.

I'm still learning ML, and I use AI tools a lot to help me understand concepts and build solutions. I know I have a lot to learn, but I'm trying my best to grow in this field.

My questions

Has anyone here transformed themselves from being lazy and undisciplined into someone who is consistent and focused?

What habits made the biggest difference in your life?

Is my 90-day plan realistic? What would you add, remove, or change?

How do you stay disciplined when your motivation disappears?

How do you avoid perfectionism while still producing high-quality work?

If you're an ML/AI engineer, what habits helped you become better at your job?

I'm not looking for shortcuts. I know changing myself will take time and effort. I just want to build a disciplined life and become someone I can be proud of.

I'd really appreciate any advice, experiences, or lessons you've learned.

Thank you!
